tool_args.raw_text: David Keller, Acting Co-Deputy Chief, Management & Budget, Consolidated Analysis reported YTD May 2024 preliminary results, compared with the Adopted Budget, were unfavorable by $83 million
the month of May was favorable by $29 million
Operating Revenues on a YTD basis were $35 million, or 1%, favorable.
Passenger revenues were $57 million unfavorable
offset by favorable toll revenue of $34 million
favorable other operating revenue of $58 million

payload.raw_value_text: favorable other operating revenue of $58 million

tool_args.raw_text: Operating Subsidies YTD through May were $148 million, or 5%, unfavorable.
Operating Expenses YTD through May were favorable by $45 million (less than 1%) favorable.

tool_args.label: MTA Finance Committee Meeting June 24, 2024
tool_args.raw_text: Minutes of the Metropolitan Transportation Authority Finance Committee Meeting 2 Broadway, 20th Floor Board Room New York, NY 10004 Monday June 24, 2024 2:30 p.m.

payload.event_name: Metropolitan Transportation Authority Finance Committee Meeting
payload.description: Finance Committee Meeting at 2 Broadway, 20th Floor Board Room, New York, NY

tool_args.raw_text: In May, operating revenues were $25 million, or 4%, favorable.
May expenses were $56 million (4%) favorable.
Debt Service Expense was $14 million (1%) unfavorable YTD through May
$16 million (8%) favorable for May, reflecting the timing of debt service accruals.
the Mortgage Recording Tax and the Urban Taxes, which collectively were unfavorable by $133 million
Payroll Mobility Tax, unfavorable by $29 million YTD through May

payload.raw_value_text: unfavorable by $29 million YTD through May

tool_args.raw_text: In March, there was a favorable accounting adjustment of $43 million

Olga Chernat, Deputy Chief, Financial Services, reported in July 2024 MTA expects to issue two series of bonds. This week, MTA is pricing Triborough Bridge and Tunnel Authority Payroll Mobility Tax Senior Lien Refunding Green Bonds, Series 2024C... in addition, later in July, MTA expects to issue Dedicated Tax Fund Bonds, Series 2024A to retire outstanding Triborough Bridge and Tunnel Authority Payroll Mobility Tax Bond Anticipation Notes, Series 2022A.

payload.description: MTA expects to issue two series of bonds: Triborough Bridge and Tunnel Authority Payroll Mobility Tax Senior Lien Refunding Green Bonds Series 2024C and Dedicated Tax Fund Bonds Series 2024A

warnings: event.organizers is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Organizer lists refer to entity records. Suggested relation: organized_by.

tool_args.raw_text: Because a quorum was not present at the June 2024 Finance Committee, Action Items were not voted on to recommend to the Board for approval; Action Items were taken directly to the full Board for approval on Wednesday, June 26, 2024.

payload.description: Action Items taken directly to the full Board for approval because quorum was not present at Finance Committee

tool_args.raw_text: Chair Zuckerman asked Deputy Chief Financial Officer Jai Patel to briefly explain how the pause in congestion pricing affects the MTA's financial plan. Ms. Patel explained areas of the budget the MTA is analyzing which are impacted by the pause including congestion pricing infrastructure, debt service costs, farebox revenues, staffing levels and operating maintenance expenses.

payload.claim_text: The pause in congestion pricing affects the MTA's financial plan including congestion pricing infrastructure, debt service costs, farebox revenues, staffing levels and operating maintenance expenses
payload.subject: congestion pricing pause

tool_args.raw_text: MTA estimates the 2024 assessment to be $1,608,341.
In 2023, the estimated 2023 assessment of $5,192,127 was more than the actual assessment of $3,598,150 by $1,593,977.
This true-up of $1,593,977 when deducted from the estimated 2024 assessment of $1,608,341, requires a total payment of $14,365

one action for MTA Headquarters for an estimated amount of $5 million. This item is the extension of a miscellaneous service contract awarded to TDX Construction Corporation ("TDX") for the Small Business Mentoring Program ("SBMP") Management for a period of up to six months (through March 31, 2025). Funding in the amount of $5 million will also be added.

payload.period: up to six months through March 31, 2025
